summary: This initiative delivers a habit tracking application that helps users build consistency through customizable reminders and progress visualization. Users can track up to 5 daily habits, receive reminders via their chosen messaging platform (SMS, Telegram, or WhatsApp), and mark habits complete either through the web interface or by replying to reminder messages. The system tracks streaks, displays progress charts, and enforces timezone-aware scheduling based on user preferences set during signup. Authentication is handled via a 6-digit code sent to the user's messaging channel.

assumptions.text: The system will use a web-based interface accessible via mobile and desktop browsers (no native mobile apps)
Users will receive reminders only through their single selected messaging channel, with no ability to receive reminders on multiple channels simultaneously
The 'current day' for habit completion is determined by the user's selected timezone, not server time
Streak calculations reset to zero when a habit is not completed on a scheduled day, rather than pausing or allowing grace periods
The habit identifier format 'xxxx' in reply messages will be system-generated and displayed in reminder messages for users to reference
User account deletion is not supported in this POC - accounts persist indefinitely once created
Messaging platform API failures during reminder delivery are acceptable for this POC - no retry logic or fallback mechanisms are required
Stopped habits cannot be restarted - users must create a new habit if they want to track something similar again

requirementsInterpreted.text: Users can create up to 5 custom daily habits with names and customizable reminder schedules
Users select one messaging channel (SMS, Telegram, or WhatsApp) during signup that cannot be changed afterward
Users can set reminder frequencies from preset options (daily, every 2 days, weekly, monthly, hourly, 6-hourly) or create custom time-of-day schedules with up to 12 specific times
Users can mark habits complete via web interface or by replying to reminder messages with the format 'Habit (xxxx): done'
The system tracks streaks per habit and sends notifications when users maintain 3+ consecutive days of completion
Progress charts visualize habit completion over the past 30 days and must load within 2 seconds
Users select a timezone during signup that determines all reminder scheduling and 'current day' calculations
Habits can only be marked complete for the current day in the user's timezone (no backdating or future-dating)
When a habit is deleted, all pending reminders for that habit are immediately cancelled
The system ignores replies to outdated reminder messages and only processes replies to the most recent reminder for each habit
Authentication is handled by sending a random 6-digit code to the user's selected messaging channel, which the user confirms to complete signup and login
Users can stop habits, which permanently cancels all future notifications for that habit without ability to restart

outOfScope.text: Social features, habit sharing between users, or community functionality
Multi-language support beyond English for messages and user interface
Offline mode, local data caching, or any functionality without active internet connection
Ability to change messaging channel after signup or receive reminders on multiple channels
Advanced analytics, AI-powered recommendations, or predictive habit insights
Retry logic or fallback mechanisms for messaging platform API failures
Ability to restart or resume stopped habits

riskFlags.text: High dependency on three external messaging APIs (SMS, Telegram, WhatsApp) with potential rate limits, costs, and reliability concerns for bi-directional communication
No stakeholders, timeline, or budget defined - project scope and resource allocation are unclear
Permanent messaging channel selection may cause user frustration if they want to switch platforms later, with no defined support process or workaround
Webhook infrastructure reliability is critical for bi-directional messaging but has no defined SLA or failover strategy
6-digit code authentication via messaging channel may have security implications and delivery reliability concerns
No account deletion means potential data retention and privacy compliance issues in the future
Stopped habits cannot be restarted, which may frustrate users who temporarily pause habits and want to resume with existing streak history
